name: Bun
what_it_does: All-in-one JavaScript toolkit that replaces Node.js, npm, Webpack, and Jest — featuring a fast runtime, bundler, package manager, and test runner in a single binary.
use_cases: Speed up agent CI pipelines by replacing npm install + ts-node with bun install + bun run for 10-20x faster cold starts
Run agent HTTP servers using Bun.serve() with built-in WebSocket support for streaming agent responses
Bundle agent tool distributions as optimized single-file ESM outputs using bun build with tree-shaking
Execute agent test suites using bun test with Jest-compatible matchers and near-instant startup
Use Bun's SQLite driver (bun:sqlite) for zero-dependency local agent state storage without installing better-sqlite3
not_for: Production Linux ARM64 environments where Bun's binary compatibility is still maturing
Teams requiring full Node.js native addon (.node files) support — many do not work with Bun's JavaScriptCore engine
Projects depending on Node.js worker_threads at scale, where Bun's threading model differs from Node's
best_when: You want the fastest possible JS/TS execution for agent scripts and are willing to accept occasional Node.js API compatibility gaps.
avoid_when: Your agent toolchain relies on packages that use Node.js-specific native bindings (node-gyp compiled modules) without ESM fallbacks.

auth.notes: No auth required for local CLI use. No managed cloud platform requiring credentials.

pricing.notes: MIT-licensed open source. No hosted service or paid tier exists as of 2024.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages include line numbers and stack traces; module resolution failures occasionally show cryptic JSC engine messages
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Script execution is stateless and idempotent by nature; bun:sqlite supports transactions for atomic writes
known_agent_gotchas: Node.js compatibility is ~95% but gaps in vm, cluster, and some crypto APIs can cause silent fallbacks that produce wrong results
bun install creates a bun.lockb binary lockfile — agents that parse lockfiles for dependency analysis need bun-specific parsers
Bun's JavaScriptCore engine handles some edge-case JS semantics differently from V8, causing subtle bugs in code relying on V8 quirks
bun compile produces platform-specific binaries — agent build pipelines must target each OS separately
Hot reload (--hot flag) can leave stale module state in memory between reloads, causing agent tools to behave inconsistently during development

security_score_components.security_notes: No built-in permission model like Deno — Bun runs with full process permissions by default. Relies on OS-level sandboxing for isolation.
